title: An elongate structural element, a bracket and an elongate structural section
abstract: There is disclosed an elongate structural element (10). The element (10) includes an inner plate (12) having first and second opposing planar surfaces (14, 16). The element (10) further includes first and second outer sections (18, 18'). Each of the outer sections (18, 18') comprises a central wall (20) having a pair of longitudinal grooves (32, 34). Each of the outer sections (18, 18') further comprises a pair of side walls (22, 24) extending from the central wall (20) to define a generally U-shaped channel (30). The longitudinal grooves (32, 34) depress into the U-shaped channel (30). Each of the outer sections (18, 18') further comprises a pair of flanges (26, 28) respectively extending outwardly from the pair of side walls (22, 24). The flanges (26, 28) of the first outer section (18) are attached to the first planar surface (14) of the plate (12) and the flanges (26, 28) of the second outer section (18') are attached to the second planar surface (16) of the plate (12) such that the U-shaped channels (30) are substantially closed by the inner plate (12) and opposed to each other.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. An elongate structural element, including: an inner plate having first and second opposing planar surfaces; and first and second outer sections, each of the outer sections comprising: a central wall having a pair of longitudinal grooves; a pair of side walls extending from the central wall to define a generally U-shaped channel, the longitudinal grooves depressing into the U-shaped channel; and a pair of flanges respectively extending outwardly from the pair of side walls, wherein the flanges of the first outer section are attached to the first planar surface of the plate and the flanges of the second outer section are attached to the second planar surface of the plate such that the U-shaped channels are substantially closed by the inner plate and opposed to each other. 2. The elongate structural element according to claim 1, wherein each of the grooves comprises a profile with an open narrow region and an enlarged end region. 3. The elongate structural element according to claim 2, wherein the profile of each groove is generally keyhole shaped. 4. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the grooves of the first outer section are substantially parallel with each other, and the grooves of the second outer section are substantially parallel with each other. 5. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the grooves of the first outer section are respectively substantially aligned with the grooves of the second outer section. 6. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the grooves of the first outer section are substantially coextensive with each other, and the grooves of the second outer section are substantially coextensive with each other. 7. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each of the central walls comprises at least one aperture. 8. The elongate structural element according to claim 7, wherein each of the apertures is generally keyhole shaped with a narrowed region and an enlarged region. 9. The elongate structural element of claim 8, wherein, in each of the keyhole shaped apertures, the narrowed region extends from the enlarged region substantially parallel to a longitudinal axis of the elongate structural element. 10. The elongate structural element of claim 8 or 9, wherein, in each of the keyhole shaped apertures, the narrowed region extends from the enlarged region at an angle with a longitudinal axis of the elongate structural element. 11. The elongate structural element of claim 10, wherein the angle is between 0 to 90 degrees. 12. The elongate structural element of claim 11, wherein the angle is about 45 degrees. 13. The elongate structural element of any one of claims 10 to 12, wherein, in each of the outer sections, the narrowed regions of the keyhole shaped apertures respectively extend from the enlarged regions of the keyhole shaped apertures in generally the same direction. 14. The elongate structural element according to claim 7, wherein each of the apertures is generally rectangular in shape. 15. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second outer sections are substantially coterminous with the inner plate. 16. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the flanges of the first outer section and the flanges of the second outer section are respectively attached to the first and second planar surfaces of the inner plate by: rivets, metal glue, seam welding, spot welding or a combination thereof. 17. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second outer sections are respectively formed from a sheet of metal, plastic, carbon fiber or a combination thereof. 18. The elongate structural element according to any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the element is formed by cast extrusion. 19. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the inner plate is formed from a sheet of metal, plastic, carbon fiber or a combination thereof. 20. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the side walls of the first outer section are respectively aligned with the side walls of the second outer section. 21. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the central wall of the first outer section is substantially parallel to the central wall of the second outer section. 22. The elongate structural element according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a plurality of attachment apertures extending through the inner plate and the flanges of the first and second outer sections. 23. An elongate structural section, including: a central wall having first and second ends; a pair of side walls extending from the central wall to define a generally U-shaped channel; and a pair of flanges respectively extending outwardly from the pair of side walls, wherein the first end has a male portion and the second end has a female portion configured to receive the male portion of a further elongate structural section such that the elongate structural section is longitudinally attached and aligned with the further elongate structural section. 24. The elongate structural section according to claim 23, wherein the male portion is generally trapezoidal in shape. 25. The elongate structural section according to claim 23 or 24, wherein the female portion is a recess corresponding to the shape of the male portion. 26. The elongate structural section according to any one of claim 23 to 25, wherein the central wall comprises at least one aperture. 27. The elongate structural section according to claim 26, wherein each of the apertures is generally keyhole shaped with a narrowed region and an enlarged region. 28. The elongate structural section according to claim 27, wherein the narrowed region extends from the enlarged region substantially parallel to a longitudinal axis of the elongate structural section. 29. The elongate structural section according to claim 28, wherein the narrowed region extends from the enlarged region substantially at an angle with a longitudinal axis of the elongate structural section. 30. The elongate structural section according to claim 29, wherein the angle is between 0 to 90 degrees. 31. The elongate structural section according to claim 30, wherein the angle is about 45 degrees. 32. The elongate structural section according to claim 26, wherein each of the apertures is generally rectangular. 33. A bracket for connecting a rail to an elongate structural element, the bracket including: a body configured for attachment to the elongate structural element, the body defining a shoulder for supporting the rail thereon; a strap configured for wrapping around the rail, the strap having first and second ends, the first end being secured to the body; and a bolt configured for attaching the second end to the body and also for fixing the location of the body relative to the elongate structural element. 34. The bracket according to claim 33, wherein the bolt is further configured for adjusting a distance between the first end and the second end to adjustably apply tension to the strap. 35. The bracket according to claim 33 or 34, further comprising a bolt aperture for receiving the bolt therethrough, the bolt aperture extending through at least the second end and the body. 36. The bracket according to claim 35, wherein the bolt aperture also extends through the first end. 37. The bracket according to any one of claims 33 to 36, wherein the bolt is receivable into an aperture of the elongate structural element. 38. The bracket according to any one of claims 33 to 37, wherein the strap comprises a sheetlike material. 39. The bracket according to claim 38, wherein the sheet-like material has a thickness between 0.05 to 0.5 mm. 40. The bracket according to claim 39, wherein the thickness of the sheet-like material is between 0.125 to 0.33 mm. 41. The bracket according to any one of claims 38 to 40, wherein the sheet-like material is formed from a metal. 42. The bracket according to any one of claims 33 to 41, wherein, when the second end is attached to the body by the bolt, the strap defines a rail aperture for holding the rail therein. 43. The bracket according to claim 42, wherein the rail aperture has a profile substantially corresponding with an outer profile of the rail. 44. The bracket according to claim 43, wherein the profile of the rail aperture is generally square, rectangular, or circular shaped. 45. The bracket according to any one of claims 33 to 44, wherein the body comprises a plate and at least one arm member extending outwardly therefrom defining the shoulder. 46. The bracket according to claim 45, wherein the arm member has a surface for abutment with the rail, the surface being substantially perpendicular to the plate. 47. The bracket according to claim 45 or 46, wherein the arm member is integrally formed with the plate. 48. The bracket according to any one of claims 45 to 47, wherein the plate comprises two end portions and the surface of the arm member is located between the two end portions. 49. The bracket according to any one of claims 45 to 48, wherein the body comprises two of said arm members. 50. The bracket according to any one of claims 33 to 49, wherein the body further comprises at least one protrusion extending from the plate portion and being receivable into another aperture of the elongate structural element for attachment. 51. The bracket according to claim 50, wherein the protrusion is a slot pin comprising a narrow portion and an enlarged end portion. 52. The bracket according to claim 50, wherein the protrusion comprises a first part extending from the plate and a second part extending from a free end of the first part to define a recess therebetween for receiving a portion of the elongate structural element.
